title: Buttery Crunchy Mediterranean Lamb Flatbread
description: A delicious and flavorful Mediterranean dish featuring slow-cooked lamb, crunchy flatbread, and a blend of aromatic spices, perfect for a brunch gathering. This paleo-friendly recipe is made with wholesome ingredients and is free from grains and dairy.
instructions: In a large Dutch oven, heat the olive oil over medium heat. Add the garlic and onion and cook until the onion is translucent.
Add the lamb shoulder to the pot and cook until browned on all sides.
Add 1 cup of water to the pot, cover, and transfer to the preheated oven. Braise the lamb for 2.5 hours, or until tender.
While the lamb is cooking, prepare the flatbread. In a large bowl, combine the cauliflower flour, almond flour, salt, black pepper, and dried oregano.
Gradually add the melted butter to the bowl and mix until a dough forms.
Divide the dough into 4-6 equal pieces, depending on desired flatbread size. Roll out each piece into a thin circle.
Once the lamb is cooked, remove it from the oven and let it rest for 10 minutes. Slice the lamb into thin strips.
Brush the flatbread with a little water and top with sliced lamb, folded to fit the bread.
Return the flatbread to the oven and bake for an additional 10-15 minutes, or until crispy and golden brown.
